name: Hugging Face Inference API
what_it_does: Hosted inference for 100,000+ open-source ML models including LLMs, embeddings, image generation, audio, and specialized NLP tasks via a unified REST API. Dedicated Endpoints provide production-grade isolated GPU inference.
use_cases: Running open-source LLM inference (Llama, Mistral, Falcon) without managing GPU infrastructure
Generating embeddings from specialized sentence-transformer models for RAG
Fine-tuned model inference for domain-specific classification, NER, or summarization
Image generation, classification, and object detection via serverless endpoints
Text-to-speech and automatic speech recognition with open models
not_for: Production workloads requiring guaranteed latency SLAs (cold starts on free tier)
Very large model inference requiring custom VRAM configurations on shared API
Teams needing dedicated isolated GPU infrastructure without Dedicated Endpoints pricing
best_when: You need to run open-source models without managing GPU infrastructure, especially for specialized tasks where open models outperform general-purpose commercial APIs.
avoid_when: You need OpenAI-level reliability guarantees, very low latency, or your model doesn't fit in the serverless tier.

auth.notes: User access tokens with scoped permissions (read, write, inference). Tokens can be restricted to specific organizations. Fine-grained tokens available for least-privilege access.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free tier: rate-limited inference on many models (cold starts, shared infrastructure, ~30 requests/hour).
pricing.paid_tiers: PRO $9/mo for faster inference and higher limits; Dedicated Endpoints from ~$0.06/hr; Enterprise custom.
pricing.notes: Free Inference API is heavily rate-limited and has cold starts. PRO unlocks faster access. Dedicated Endpoints are billed per GPU-hour and are production-grade with no cold starts.

performance.rate_limits: Free: ~30 requests/hour; PRO: higher limits undocumented; Dedicated: no shared limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Errors include error field with description and estimated_time for model loading. 503 with estimated_time is common for cold starts and is a normal operational state agents must handle. Documentation sometimes lags model updates.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Inference is stateless — same inputs produce same outputs (for deterministic models). No deduplication or idempotency key mechanism.
known_agent_gotchas: Model cold start: first request after idle returns 503 with estimated_time — must implement retry with exponential backoff
Model-specific input formats vary widely — agents must read model cards before calling an unfamiliar model
Some models require Pro subscription or are gated (require explicit HF account approval)
Free Inference API availability is not guaranteed for all models — check model page for availability status
Response schema differs by task type (text-generation vs feature-extraction vs fill-mask) — no unified response envelope
Dedicated Endpoints have separate URLs and separate credentials from the shared Inference API

score_rationale: Hugging Face Inference API provides unmatched breadth of open-source model access with a straightforward REST interface, earning solid fq_score. However, cold starts, rate limits on free tier, per-model schema variation, and gated model friction significantly impact agent reliability — hence the lower af_score. Dedicated Endpoints (billed separately) substantially improve agent suitability but require additional setup and cost. Scoped tokens are a plus for security.

security_score_components.security_notes: User access tokens with fine-grained scopes (read, write, infer). Models from community — vet before production use. Model cards for transparency.
